meta.title: 11.6 Checkpoint
meta.description: Design a data pipeline for a real-time analytics dashboard processing millions of events per second.

blocks.content:
## Level 11 Checkpoint

Design data processing systems.

blocks.question: A ride-sharing company needs: (1) real-time driver location updates on a map (sub-second), (2) trip cost calculation at ride completion, (3) weekly city-level analytics (average trip time, popular routes). Design the data pipeline.
blocks.answer:
**Real-time location (Kappa-style stream):** Driver apps publish GPS events to Kafka. A Flink job processes location updates in real-time and pushes to Redis (geospatial index). The rider app reads from Redis via WebSocket for live map updates.

**Trip cost (stream processing):** Trip-completed events from Kafka → Flink job calculates cost (distance × rate + surge + time). Writes result to PostgreSQL (trip record) and publishes TripCompleted event.

**Weekly analytics (batch):** CDC from PostgreSQL → Kafka → S3 (raw events, Parquet format). Weekly Spark job reads from S3, computes city-level aggregations, writes to a data warehouse (BigQuery/Redshift). Dashboards read from warehouse.

**Architecture:** Kappa for real-time, batch supplement for heavy analytics. Kafka as the central event bus. CDC keeps the analytics pipeline in sync without modifying the application.

blocks.content:
## Ready for Level 12?

If you can design data pipelines combining batch and stream processing with appropriate architectures, you are ready. Level 12 covers the security layer.
